assumption_floor.name: Poseidon2-permutation collision-resistance
assumption_floor.detail: the arithmetization-friendly hash; the sponge/Merkle/state-commitment collision-resistance is reduced to permutation-CR (`Crypto.Poseidon2*`, `Crypto.Merkle`, the `recStateCommit` injectivity portal).

assumption_floor.name: FRI / the STARK soundness chain
assumption_floor.detail: a verifying proof attests its statement; the one recursion obligation `RecursiveAggregation.EngineSound.recursive_sound`.

assumption_floor.name: PostGSTProgress
assumption_floor.detail: the network is eventually synchronous (after GST); the consensus LIVENESS carrier (`World.gst_liveness`, derived from a DLS88/HotStuff `Pacemaker`).

guarantees.title: AUTHORITY
guarantees.statement: Every state change is justified by an unforgeable, non-amplified, fresh token chain.
guarantees.floor: ed25519 (signature on the handoff), HMAC (caveat-chain tags), Poseidon2-CR (in-circuit cap-root openings). No trusted "this was authorized" premise survives.

keystones.name: AuthModes.captp_granted_le_held
keystones.summary: the dispatcher GATE: when a CapTP-delivered handoff admits, `granted.rights ≤ held.rights`. This is the `is_attenuation(held, granted)` check dregg1's `verify_captp_delivered` FAILS to perform; the gate certifies it.

keystones.name: AuthModes.captp_sound
keystones.summary: / `bearer_sound` / `token_sound` — the per-mode soundness refinements: admission + the abstract premises discharge a Granovetter `Introduce` / delegation edge / discharged-token verify-seam.

keystones.name: EffectsAuthority.introduce_non_amplifying
keystones.summary: the HEADLINE over the real `List Auth` attenuation lattice: a conferred cap is a genuine SUBSET of the held cap.

keystones.name: EffectsAuthority.amplifying_grant_rejected
keystones.summary: the TEETH: a grant conferring authority the holder lacks is REJECTED (the predicate is two-valued, not `:= True`).

keystones.summary: (the Spec-level capability graph) closes the loop.

guarantees.title: CONSERVATION
guarantees.statement: Per asset, the resource sum is exactly zero across a turn (and a run).
guarantees.floor: NONE beyond integer arithmetic. Conservation is a kernel theorem; the only crypto that touches it is Pedersen (DLog) IF values are committed rather than cleartext, and the case proves committed = cleartext via `Spec.committed_iff_cleartext`.

keystones.name: RecordKernel.recTransferBal_sum_conserve_moved
keystones.summary: over the GENUINE multi-asset ledger `CellId → AssetId → ℤ`: a transfer of asset `a` leaves `∑_c bal c a` invariant (the moved asset's total supply is exactly conserved).

keystones.name: RecordKernel.recTransferBal_untouched
keystones.summary: an UNtouched asset `b ≠ a` is pointwise unchanged (no cross-asset leakage: the per-asset sums are independent).

keystones.name: RecordKernel.recKExec_conserves
keystones.summary: the executor step conserves the balance sum.

keystones.name: Spec.turnConserves_balance
keystones.summary: / `Spec.conservation_over_monoid` — the value-monoid -parametric whole-turn conservation (committed = cleartext value).

keystones.name: Conserve.sum_transfer_conserve
keystones.summary: the shared library lemma the above rest on; with the honesty rail `Conserve.sum_transfer_conserve` genuinely requiring `src ≠ dst`.

guarantees.title: INTEGRITY
guarantees.statement: A receipt binds the WHOLE post-state; a tampered input is rejected.
guarantees.floor: Poseidon2-permutation-CR (the `recStateCommit`/`cellCommit`/`stateCommit` injectivity portals reduce to it). A second pre-image would be the only way to forge a receipt for a different state; that is exactly the CR assumption.

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.Receipt.argus_commits_to_one_receipt
keystones.summary: THE connection keystone: the Argus circuit term commits to exactly one receipt, determined by the post-state.

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.Receipt.argus_circuit_executor_receipts_agree
keystones.summary: the cross-corner: the circuit's receipt and the executor's receipt AGREE (one semantics, two readings).

keystones.name: Circuit.CommitmentCrossBind.runnable_binds_same_system_roots
keystones.summary: the state-commitment binds cells AND the rest-of-state: equal seam roots ⇒ equal full state.

keystones.name: Circuit.CommitmentCrossBind.chC_bad_not_bridge
keystones.summary: the TEETH: a commitment that drops a field is NOT a faithful bridge (so the binding is not vacuous; a tampered field can be detected because the honest commitment recovers it).

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.Receipt.transfer_commits_to_one_receipt
keystones.summary: the transfer weld instance.

guarantees.title: FRESHNESS
guarantees.statement: No replay / double-spend; a committed spend's nullifier was fresh; revocation at finality.
guarantees.floor: Poseidon2-CR (the nullifier-set sorted-tree root openings). PostGSTProgress for the revocation-at-finality leg (consensus terminates after GST).

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.noteSpendStmt_no_double_spend
keystones.summary: IN THE TERM: if the noteSpend term COMMITS, the spent nullifier was NOT already in the set (`nf ∉ nullifiers`). The anti-replay is the primitive's own `interp`, not an out-of-band executor side table.

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.noteSpendStmt_then_reject
keystones.summary: the composed barrier: after a committed spend of `nf`, a second spend of the SAME `nf` fails closed.

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.noteSpendStmt_replay_rejected
keystones.summary: the TEETH (witness FALSE): a nullifier already present is REJECTED (`= none`); the gate is genuinely two-valued.

keystones.name: Crypto.NonMembership.nonmembership_sound
keystones.summary: / `_complete` — the freshness witness is a sorted-tree non-membership opening: `nf ∉ set` is a whole-set assertion with a circuit gate (closes the "in-memory only" gap).

keystones.name: Liveness.revocation_needs_consensus
keystones.summary: revocation is consensus-bound: it takes effect when (and only when) all relevant views agree the epoch advanced (immediate AT finality, the negative-lifecycle dual of consensus-free GC).

guarantees.title: UNFOOLABILITY
guarantees.statement: A light client verifying a Q-chain learns A–D for the WHOLE history; re-witnessing nothing.
guarantees.floor: FRI / STARK soundness (`EngineSound.recursive_sound`, the ONE recursion obligation), Poseidon2-CR (`recStateCommit` binds the seam roots), ed25519 (strand-block signatures), PostGSTProgress (a FINALIZED — not merely valid — chain, via the finality-cert leg).

keystones.name: Circuit.RecursiveAggregation.light_client_verifies_whole_history
keystones.summary: THE headline: checking ONLY `verify agg.root` (re-witnessing NOTHING) ⇒ every turn executed correctly, correctly ordered, and the final root is a genuine fold. Proofs-as-additive-attestation.

keystones.name: Circuit.RecursiveAggregation.attested_history_conserves
keystones.summary: the whole attested history conserves (guarantee B, lifted to the full run).

keystones.name: Circuit.RecursiveAggregation.tampered_aggregate_cannot_bind
keystones.summary: the ANTI-GHOST: a reordered chain forces `ChainBound = False`, so no verifying aggregate exists.

keystones.name: Circuit.RecursiveAggregation.leaf_pairing_defeats_swap
keystones.summary: positional pairing means a verifying leaf is not re-pointable to a different step.

keystones.name: Distributed.HistoryAggregation.wellformed_attests_whole_history
keystones.summary: the IVC fold model: the seam tooth `new_root[i] = old_root[i+1]` pins the whole history; `root_tooth_pins_state` is the CR recovery (a light client seeing only roots learns state continuity).

keystones.name: Circuit.Argus.Aggregate.argus_strand_light_client
keystones.summary: + `tampered_argus_strand_rejected` — the Argus-strand realization of the same case on the executable term IR.

guarantees.title: THE RUNNING ENTRY (A∧B∧C hold over what the node actually runs)
guarantees.statement: The five guarantees above are stated over the abstract kernel: the `List Auth` attenuation lattice (A), the multi-asset ledger `recTransferBal` (B), the Argus term IR (C/D), the aggregation fold (E). The honest question ember keeps pressing is: *do those guarantees hold over the executor the deployed node INVOKES* — `execFullForestG`, the body behind the `dregg_exec_full_forest_auth` FFI export (`Exec.FFI`), the one `produce_via_lean` / `lean_shadow` call? They do, and this section proves it in ONE statement rather than leaving it as a reader's inference.
